summary: Soundboard recording with occasional mix drop‑outs captures a strong LA Coliseum show highlighted by a ripping Deal, rare Bertha > Greatest Story segue and solid second‑set jams.
review: The sbd source provides generally clear audio, though reviewers note a brief moment where Jerry drops out of the mix and some first‑set PA hiccups. Musically the concert is praised for its powerful Shakedown Street opener, an uncommon Bertha > Greatest Story transition, a ten‑minute plus Deal, and a second set packed with strong Foolish Heart, Uncle John’s Band and other jams. The only real blemish mentioned is a dislike of Picasso Moon.

ai_rating.rationale: Show receives high praise with many standout moments, while the soundboard recording is solid but has minor mix drop‑outs; weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall clear sbd audio, but occasional mix issues (Jerry’s brief disappearance) and first‑set PA problems are noted.

standout_songs: Bertha > The Greatest Story Ever Told
show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic first set with a stellar Shakedown opener and rare Bertha > Greatest Story pairing; Set I closes on an expansive Deal. Second set delivers long, cohesive jams (UJB, Miracle, Not Fade Away) with a solid Rhythm Devils > Space segment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Briefly drops out of the mix during the first set but otherwise delivers ripping guitar throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Rhythm Devils > Space described as well‑played and characteristic of the era.
